questions.question: Prove that the lengths of tangents drawn from an external point to a circle are equal.

steps: Draw a circle with center O and an external point P.
Draw two tangents PA and PB to the circle from P.
Join OA, OB and OP.
Consider triangles OAP and OBP.
Show that the two triangles are congruent using RHS congruence criterion.
Conclude that PA = PB by CPCT.
solution.derivation: In \triangle OAP \text{ and } \triangle OBP: \\ 1. OA = OB \text{ (radii of the same circle)} \\ 2. \angle OAP = \angle OBP = 90^\circ \text{ (tangent is perpendicular to radius)} \\ 3. OP = OP \text{ (common side)} \\ \therefore \triangle OAP \cong \triangle OBP \text{ (RHS Criterion)} \\ \implies PA = PB \text{ (CPCT)}

questions.question: A quadrilateral $ABCD$ is drawn to circumscribe a circle. Prove that $AB + CD = AD + BC$.

steps: Use the property that tangents from an external point to a circle are equal in length.
Label the points of contact as P, Q, R, S on sides AB, BC, CD, DA respectively.
Express segments of the sides in terms of these tangent lengths.
Sum the sides AB + CD and AD + BC.
Show that both sums result in the same algebraic expression.
solution.derivation: Let points of contact be P, Q, R, S. \\ AP=AS, BP=BQ, CR=CQ, DR=DS \\ AB+CD = (AP+PB) + (CR+DR) \\ = (AS+BQ) + (CQ+DS) \\ = (AS+DS) + (BQ+CQ) \\ = AD + BC

questions.question: Two concentric circles are of radii 5 cm and 3 cm. Find the length of the chord of the larger circle which touches the smaller circle.

steps: Draw a diagram with concentric circles center O.
Let AB be the chord of the larger circle touching smaller circle at P.
OP is radius of smaller circle, OA is radius of larger circle.
OP is perpendicular to AB.
Apply Pythagoras theorem in \triangle OPA.
Calculate AP and double it to find chord length AB.
solution.derivation: OA^2 = OP^2 + AP^2 \\ 5^2 = 3^2 + AP^2 \\ 25 = 9 + AP^2 \\ AP^2 = 16 \implies AP = 4 \text{ cm} \\ AB = 2 \times AP = 8 \text{ cm}

questions.question: If a hexagon $ABCDEF$ circumscribes a circle, prove that $AB + CD + EF = BC + DE + FA$.
questions.answer: AB + CD + EF = BC + DE + FA

steps: Identify that tangents from the same exterior point to a circle are equal.
Let the points of contact be P, Q, R, S, T, U on sides AB, BC, CD, DE, EF, FA.
Write equations for each vertex: AP=AU, BP=BQ, CQ=CR, DR=DS, ES=ET, FT=FU.
Sum the segments for both sides of the equation.
Equate and simplify to prove the result.
